summary: close() with parallelCollectionScan() segfaults upon request shutdown
2.6.1-rc0 requires $gt for OplogReplay
w=0 can return an unexpected exception on failure
collection->update($query, $document) crashes if $document is an object
description:
This worked fine with mongo 2.4, now with mongo 2.6 I only get 502 bad gateway (with nginx) if i try to update the whole document.

{code}
->update(
  array(
    '_id' => $document->_id
  ),
  $document
);
{code}
          
$document is a somehow complex object. What works for now is to use

{code}
json_decode(json_encode($document), true)
{code}

although it is slower.

$document:

{code}

  public '_id' => int 2
  public 'notifications' =>

summary: Read timed out after reading 0 bytes, waited for 0.000000 seconds
description:
After upgrading from mongo 2.2.4 / PHP driver 1.3.7 to versions 2.4.6 / 1.4.4, we started seeing this connection error intermittently when running a find query on a sharded replicaset (mongos running locally).  We've been able to reproduce it with drivers 1.4.2 and 1.4.0.  When reverting to driver 1.3.7, the error disappears.  So it appears to be something introduced in the 1.4 driver (?)

It appears to happen unpredictably, which of course makes it mildly maddening. 

Happy to provide additional info or details, please let me know.

summary: Coverity analysis defect 10700: Explicit null dereferenced
description:
A new defect has been detected and assigned to bjori in Coverity Connect.
              http://coverity.mongodb.com//sourcebrowser.htm?projectId=10008#mergedDefectId=10700 
              The defect was flagged by checker FORWARD_NULL in 
                    file /gridfs/gridfs.c 
                    function zim_MongoGridFS_storeFile 
              and this ticket was created by bjori

summary: Coverity analysis defect 22173: Dereference null return value
description:
A new defect has been detected and assigned to bjori in Coverity Connect.
              http://coverity.mongodb.com//sourcebrowser.htm?projectId=10008#mergedDefectId=22173 
              The defect was flagged by checker NULL_RETURNS in 
                    file /collection.c 
                    function zim_MongoCollection_count 
              and this ticket was created by bjori

summary: Inserting documents with WriteConcern during failover broken
description:
Inserting a document during failover should throw exception unless you set w=0.

This fails as of https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/684
which was fixing w=0 inserts to _not_ throw exception - but it went a bit far and forgot to check the write concern after get_server().

There has not been a release of the driver since it broke, so it does not affect any current releases.

summary: Standalone connection to arbiter fails
description:
When connecting with a simple standalone connection string (ie. {{mongodb://hostname:port/}}) to an arbiter, the MongoClient constructor throws
{noformat}
Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'No candidate servers found' in test.php:11
{noformat}
This is in contrast to other drivers (eg. Python) and the {{mongo}} shell, which can make standalone connections to arbiters (usually for monitoring purposes).

It seems that the driver is applying the replica set logic for discovering servers, which is not appropriate for standalone connections.  Below is a verbose trace:
{noformat}
Notice: PARSE   INFO: Parsing mongodb://localhost:10684/ in test.php on line 11
Notice: PARSE   INFO: - Found node: localhost:10684 in test.php on line 11
Notice: PARSE   INFO: - Connection type: STANDALONE in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     INFO: mongo_get_read_write_connection: finding a STANDALONE connection in test.php on line 11

Notice: CON     INFO: stream_connect: Not establishing SSL for localhost:10684 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     INFO: get_server_flags: start in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     FINE: send_packet: read from header: 36 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     FINE: send_packet: data_size: 381 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     FINE: get_server_flags: setting maxBsonObjectSize to 16777216 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     FINE: get_server_flags: setting maxMessageSizeBytes to 48000000 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     INFO: get_server_flags: found server type: ARBITER in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     INFO: is_ping: pinging localhost:10684;-;.;14787 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     FINE: send_packet: read from header: 36 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     FINE: send_packet: data_size: 17 in test.php on line 11
Notice: CON     INFO: is_ping: last pinged at 1392178949; time: 0ms in test.php on line 11
Notice: REPLSET FINE: finding candidate servers in test.php on line 11
Notice: REPLSET FINE: - all servers in test.php on line 11
Notice: REPLSET FINE: filter_connections: adding connections: in test.php on line 11
Notice: REPLSET FINE: filter_connections: done in test.php on line 11

Notice: REPLSET FINE: limiting by seeded/discovered servers: done in test.php on line 11
Notice: REPLSET FINE: limiting by credentials in test.php on line 11
Notice: REPLSET FINE: limiting by credentials: done in test.php on line 11

summary: Travis build status incorrect in v1.5 branch
description:
README.md in v1.5 contains
[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-php-driver.png?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-php-driver)

Which points to master, therefore causing incorrect build status indicator.

The CONTRIBUTING.md file says the branch is called 1.5, not v1.5

summary: Allow NumberLong numbers on 32-bit platforms if the number fits in the 32-bit int range
description: Right now, when any stored NumberLong is encountered when marshaling to PHP it is blocked on 32bit systems. Instead, it should be allows if the number fits in the 32bit int range.

summary: 32bit platform improvements
description: There are various tests that are failing on 32bit systems, and MongoCursor->info() and MongoCommandCursor->info() are also not usable on 32bit platforms due to the cursor ID being 64bit.

summary: Min server version check should use GTE comparison
description: The {{php_mongo_api_connection_min_server_version()}} function was introduced here: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/607/files#diff-c3dcc64c18e6676b8e04658080943b08R54

The code in question iterates over a cursor and updates the documents. The cursor is rather big (few million results) - never had that error with smaller cursors. It happens randomly - the same code can cause it once and run again just fine.

I'm running Ubuntu Server 12.04 from Amazon AWS AMI with PHP 5.5 from Ondriej PPA (https://launchpad.net/~ondrej/+archive/php5) and Mongo driver from Pecl.

summary: MongoCursor::$timeout=-1 doesn't work anymore
description:
I upgraded MongoDB to 2.6 today and the PHP Driver to 1.5.1. Immediately after connection and before doing any queries I set MongoCursor::$timeout to -1. This used to prevent any timeouts in earlier versions. Now with the new MongoDB and driver I get:

MongoCursorTimeoutException, Read timed out after reading 0 bytes, waited for 30.00000 seconds

Now, when looking this up in the docs I find a (IMHO) highly confusing mix of different settings, deprecated settings and the like. I wonder:

1) Where does the default timeout of 30 seconds come from? Should there still be a default timeout with current versions at all? Where is the documentation for this?
2) How can I disable this globally (NOT for each query separately)? Interestingly, setting $timeout to a value other than -1 works as expected but yields a deprecation warning. -1 doesnt cause a deprecation warning - but doesn't work. I might, of course, set $timeout to a very high value but I'd rather get to the bottom of this.

summary: Replace JSON encoding of stream notifications with standard PHP serialize encoding
description:
Running "pecl install mongo" installs version 1.5.0

Running php cli returns warning:
PHP Warning:  PHP Startup: Unable to load dynamic library '/usr/lib64/php/modules/mongo.so' - /usr/lib64/php/modules/mongo.so: undefined symbol: php_json_encode in Unknown on line 0

Running php -i via CLI phpinfo() via GUI returns no mongo module

Downgrading to mongo driver version 1.4.5 resolves issue

summary: New Replica Set connections hang when secondaries are creating indexes during initialSync
php driver throws exception when connect string contains an unresolvable hostname
description:
When the mongodb connect string contains an unresolvable hostname, the php mongo driver v1.4.5 will print PHP warning, "Warning: MongoClient::__construct(): php_network_getaddresses: getaddrinfo failed: Name or service not known ".   It will proceed to work properly, so long as any seed host in the connect string is reachable.

In the 1.5.2 driver, it will throw an exception, like this:  exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'MongoClient::__construct(): php_network_getaddresses: getaddrinfo failed: Name or service not known' in ...

If the connect string contains a resolvable but unreachable hostname, then no error is generated at all (this is expected behavior).

The expected behavior should be that if any seed hosts are reachable, then MongoClient() connects successfully.    Otherwise, HA is compromised: if one of my secondaries has a bad DNS entry, then the entire replica set becomes unreachable.   

We have run into this problem in production when doing routine maintenance on a secondary which included changing its DNS name, causing the unintended consequence of bringing the entire replica set down.

summary: MongoWriteBatch::add() should accept array or object argument
Upsert should return _id instead of array of ids
socketTimeoutMS=-1 doesn't work
description: The recommended way of setting socket timeout via socketTimeoutMS option of MongoClient does not work when setting it to -1 as it validates the value to be > 0

summary: Explain how to_index_string() creates names for ensureIndex() and deleteIndex()
description: The [MongoCollection::deleteIndex()|http://php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.deleteindex.php] documentation, for all of its verbosity, doesn't explain that it simply appends "_1" to a string argument to delete an index by name. That would make it very clear as to why it is not suited to delete custom index names. Mentioning that {{ensureIndex()}} does the same for its string arguments would also allow users to realize the logic for both methods support string arguments (e.g. one could easily ensure/delete an index by simple field name, and an ascending index would be created and then deleted).

summary: Connection is not persitent with replica set and run on Linux
Latency in replica set connection in Linux mongo and PHP
description:
I have tested with 2 applications (C# and PHP) to send a request and get a response string from 5 servers(1 primary and 4 secondary) located in different continents using the same connection string (readPreference=secondaryPreffered).
For the C# application, I can get the response within 2-3 seconds, but for the PHP I get the same response within 12-15 seconds. So we know it's the PHP-mongo driver that causes the issue.
Package analyses reveal that in C# application, it first starts to grab the nearest secondary storage and then check for authentication; it later pings other servers to make sure they are alive before replying the client with a response. Meanwhile, in PHP application the implementation becomes totally different, it first grabs the server specified first in the connection string and verifies for authentication information even thought this first one is primary storage. Then it pings the next server to verify its status and again checks for its authentication with a request/reply of the client/server system information. This repeats until it completes all servers to finally pick the nearest one to send its response to.

Could someone please take a look into this because its optimization on replica set for PHP-mongo driver seems to have not been well done with thorough tests over remote servers ?

Regards,
-Adam ;)

summary: MongoClient constructor crashes on connection to slave
Aggregate cursor on non-existing collection throws weird error
The mongo driver fails to build as static extension
description:
The changelog doesn't mention anything about dropping PHP 5.3.x support. However, I was unable to make a successful build with 1.5.0, 1.5.2, and 1.5.3. The last stable version that builds successfully is 1.4.5.

The extension is built into the PHP binary by our build system since it's easier to deploy than using shared libs. A small script to reproduce the issue (under OS X):

#!/usr/bin/env bash

export PHP_AUTOCONF=autoconf213

wget http://us1.php.net/get/php-5.3.28.tar.bz2/from/this/mirror -O php-5.3.28.tar.bz2
wget http://pecl.php.net/get/mongo-1.5.3.tgz

tar -xf php-5.3.28.tar.bz2
tar -xf mongo-1.5.3.tgz

mv mongo-1.5.3 php-5.3.28/ext/mongo
cd php-5.3.28

./buildconf --force
./configure --enable-mongo
make

The build exits with this error:

main/internal_functions.c:71:2: error: use of undeclared identifier
      'phpext_mongo_ptr'
        phpext_mongo_ptr,
        ^
main/internal_functions.c:93:57: error: invalid application of 'sizeof' to an
      incomplete type 'zend_module_entry *[]'
        return php_register_extensions(php_builtin_extensions, EXTCOUNT TSRMLS...
                                                               ^~~~~~~~
main/internal_functions.c:89:25: note: expanded from macro 'EXTCOUNT'
#define EXTCOUNT (sizeof(php_builtin_extensions)/sizeof(zend_module_entry *))
                        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
2 errors generated.
make: *** [main/internal_functions.lo] Error 1

Same thing happens under Ubuntu 12.04 with gcc 4.6.3.

summary: Implement fluent userland Batch Write API
description: The new MongoWriteBatch classes need a userland helper library to implement fancy fluent interface on top of those.

summary: Journal option should set "j" field in GLE instead of "journal"
description: I believe this bug traces back to [this commit|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/eca72c3108279594c934cbc0d847c27c34c2853a#diff-bbb63725ba5691665c462960d8913e39R371], which was implemented for PHP-572.

summary: MongoInt64 no longer work or has changed in the DB
Document deprecated MongoClient properties
description:
In your Php Doc:
http://www.php.net/manual/en/class.mongoclient.php#mongoclient.props.connected

In my page ... :
*Deprecated*: Mongo_Model::checkConnection(): The 'connected' property is deprecated in /var/www/MongoDB/application/modules/beMongo/models/mongo.php on line 345

It's not mentionned as deprecated in the doc...

Thanks in advance.

summary: MongoCollection::find() doesn't works over MongoDate field, if an index created for this field
Previous connection attempts failed
description:
When mongo is shutdown and started again, the old persistent connections throws an exception until apache or php is restarted:

E_ERROR: Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'Failed to connect to: 127.0.0.1:27017: Previous connection attempts failed, server blacklisted.

In develop environment (MacBook Pro) happens the same.

summary: Add support for ARM architecture
MongoId $id property is not documented correctly
Clean up GridFS insert/delete method documentation
description: This documentation is missing options from the MongoCollection write methods and has some other inaccuracies that should be corrected.

summary: "Available since" documentation for options arrays
description:
It would be great to have "(since 1.4.0)"-type documentation for options accepted by MongoClient and MongoCollection methods, for example:

http://us3.php.net/manual/en/mongoclient.construct.php

It's not the least bit obvious which options are available as of the current stable release, which are documentation for the upcoming 1.5.0 release, and how long any of them have been available.

The docbook format does not seem to support versioning for options arrays, but really, anything would be better than what we have now. Simply manually appending "(since X)" to the end of each option's documentation would be preferable. Basically, I'd welcome anything to keep me from having to manually git bisect the driver codebase and read the source :)

summary: Clarify how "explain" option conflicts with MongoCollection::aggregateCursor()
long_as_object = 0 no effect
Clean up MONGO_METHOD calls in db_ref.c
Clean up MONGO_METHOD calls in mongo.c
Segfault when passing null value to MongoCollection::find() and unclear other conditions are met, inaccurate error message
description:
We had a bug in my MongoDB wrapper where null was sometimes passed to MongoCollection::find(), which typically generated this error:

{quote}
FastCGI sent in stderr: "PHP message: PHP Warning:  MongoCollection::find() expects parameter 1 to be MongoClient, null given in ...
{quote}

This appears to be incorrect, because it actually wants {{array $query = array()}}, not MongoClient.

However, based on apparently unrelated changes elsewhere in the code this condition would sometimes cause a segfault/zend_mm_heap corruption:

{quote}
#0  0x0000000000884a87 in zend_parse_arg_impl (arg_num=1, arg=0x4c73988, va=0x7fff1bb610a0, spec=0x7fff1bb60fe0, error=0x7fff1bb60f68, severity=0x7fff1bb60f8c)
    at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_API.c:558
#1  0x0000000000885218 in zend_parse_arg (arg_num=1, arg=0x4c73988, va=0x7fff1bb610a0, spec=0x7fff1bb60fe0, quiet=0) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_API.c:675
#2  0x0000000000885913 in zend_parse_va_args (num_args=3, type_spec=0x7f705fc02ed8 "Os|zz", va=0x7fff1bb610a0, flags=0) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_API.c:844
#3  0x0000000000885bf6 in zend_parse_parameters (num_args=4, type_spec=0x7f705fc02ed8 "Os|zz") at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_API.c:895
#4  0x00007f705fbc7a38 in zim_MongoCursor___construct (ht=4, return_value=0x7fff1bb61280, return_value_ptr=0x0, this_ptr=0x4cf9628, return_value_used=0) at /opt/php-debug/tmp/mongo/cursor.c:233
#5  0x00007f705fbd3f1b in zim_MongoCollection_find (ht=2, return_value=0x4cf9628, return_value_ptr=0x7fff1bb618a8, this_ptr=0x4cf92a8, return_value_used=1) at /opt/php-debug/tmp/mongo/collection.c:738
#6  0x000000000086ea3e in zend_call_function (fci=0x7fff1bb61820, fci_cache=0x7fff1bb61870) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_execute_API.c:980
#7  0x00000000007404e1 in zif_call_user_func_array (ht=2, return_value=0x4cf95b0, return_value_ptr=0x0, this_ptr=0x0, return_value_used=1) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/ext/standard/basic_functions.c:4754
#8  0x00000000008c1118 in zend_do_fcall_common_helper_SPEC (execute_data=0x7f70664cd890) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_vm_execute.h:642
#9  0x00000000008c838c in ZEND_DO_FCALL_SPEC_CONST_HANDLER (execute_data=0x7f70664cd890) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_vm_execute.h:2223
#10 0x00000000008bfa02 in execute (op_array=0x7f705c5b29e0) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend_vm_execute.h:410
#11 0x0000000000881d83 in zend_execute_scripts (type=8, retval=0x0, file_count=3) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/Zend/zend.c:1315
#12 0x00000000007f5250 in php_execute_script (primary_file=0x7fff1bb64210) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/main/main.c:2492
#13 0x00000000009d81ac in main (argc=3, argv=0x7fff1bb66488) at /home/ark/php5-debug/php5-5.4.13/sapi/fpm/fpm/fpm_main.c:1924
{quote}

This could be reproduced consistently across a set of machines with php versions 5.4.11-5.4.13 (current) and mongo driver 1.3.2-1.3.6 (current).

Unfortunately, I can't produce a minimal script to reproduce, because the segfault would only occur in the context of the larger codebase, but maybe this backtrace can be useful?

The segfaults stopped once I added a no-op (assigned a value to an unused variable) at the top of the file including my wrapper. This could also be consistently reproduced (i.e. reverting the change makes it crash again in MongoCollection_find).

(fixing the issue with nulls being passed also fixes the segfaults)

summary: Connections to server hangs when indexes are being created
Fix documentation for MongoCursor::timeout() default
description: http://php.net/manual/en/mongocursor.timeout.php should list the default as "30 seconds".

summary: Driver emits warnings after setting connectTimeoutMS
php_mongo_io_stream_read() should revert to default_socket_timeout if socketTimeoutMS was zero
description: Currently, {{php_mongo_io_stream_read()}} reverts to {{socketTimeoutMS}} from a temporary timeout value. If {{socketTimeoutMS}} was zero to begin with, this means we leave the socket stream in an inconsistent state.

summary: Use default and block indefinitely for zero and negative connect/socket timeouts, respectively
Update documentation for MongoLog constants
